summary.label: Normalized events
summary.detail: WAF, IDS, and DDoS JSONL samples were normalized into one shift-ready watchboard.

summary.label: Blocked or contained
summary.detail: Block, challenge, mitigate, and isolate outcomes are separated from pure escalation noise.

summary.label: Human escalations
summary.detail: Only the events that survived validation moved into analyst or owner escalation.

summary.label: Tuned detections
summary.detail: The sample shift demonstrates how pattern tuning becomes part of normal response work.

lanes.label: DDoS lane
lanes.detail: Mitigation, challenge, and blackhole watch decisions stayed tied to service continuity rather than raw traffic alone.

lanes.label: IDS lane
lanes.detail: East-west validation reduced noisy internal detections down to the few signals worth escalation.

lanes.label: WAF lane
lanes.detail: Credential stuffing, SQLi, path traversal, and virtual-patch hits concentrated on exposed service edges.

patterns.label: L7 GET flood
patterns.detail: Attack pressure was heavy enough to justify mitigation but still needed real-user success tracking.

patterns.label: Credential-stuffing burst
patterns.detail: Repeated login abuse across multiple ASNs suggests coordinated bot reuse rather than one noisy source.

patterns.label: Bot retry spillover
patterns.detail: Important signal retained for analyst validation and follow-through.

patterns.label: East-west beacon
patterns.detail: One chain stayed important only after suppression removed obvious noise.

handoff.label: APAC game launch edge
handoff.detail: Do not relax the blackhole trigger until the retry-bot slope stays below baseline for one more watch window.

handoff.label: JP login edge
handoff.detail: ASN-based tuning reduced attack pressure, but the narrow mobile-carrier exception review should stay open for the next shift.

handoff.label: Cloud console route
handoff.detail: The temporary WAF rule should remain online until the platform owner confirms patch rollout and change-unfreeze criteria.
